compiled_state.title: Isolate session_log from graph/health compile paths and default list
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
Keep verbose `session_log` bodies out of the dependency-graph and store-health hot
paths, and out of default `list`, while keeping them in `search` and `show`.

## Why

`reduce_all_tickets()` compiles full bodies with no lazy-loading and backs
`ready`, `next_batch`, `deps`, `validate`, and `list`. Verbose logs must not tax
those. The requester chose "searchable, hidden from list": logs appear in keyword
`search` and `show` but nowhere that would slow the constant graph/health work.

## What

- Add an `exclude_ticket_types` (or `exclude_session_logs`) parameter to
  `reduce_all_tickets()` in `src/rebar/reducer/_api.py`, mirroring the existing
  `exclude_archived` / `exclude_deleted` pattern.
- Default it on for graph/health callers: `src/rebar/graph/_ready.py`
  (`find_ready_tickets`), `src/rebar/graph/_graph.py` (`build_dep_graph`),
  `src/rebar/_engine_support/next_batch.py` (`compute`), and
  `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate.py` (`validate_state` health checks).
- Exclude `session_log` from default `list` (surface only on explicit
  `--type=session_log`) in `src/rebar/_engine_support/reads.py` /
  `src/rebar/reducer/_filters.py`.
- Keep `session_log` in the `search` corpus (`src/rebar/reducer/search.py`) and in
  `show` (single-ticket reduce path unchanged).

## Scope

Read-path visibility and graph/health isolation only. The recent-logs command is
a separate story.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ready`, `next_batch`, `deps`, and `validate` never include `session_log`
      tickets and their timings are unaffected by log count/size (asserted).
- [ ] Default `list` omits `session_log`; `list --type=session_log` returns them.
- [ ] `search <keywords>` matches `session_log` bodies; `show` returns a
      `session_log` fully.
- [ ] `validate` does not flag `session_log` tickets as orphaned/empty/etc.
- [ ] Tests assert both the exclusions and the inclusions.

comments.body:
HANDOFF CHECKPOINT (session-log loop, session boundary). Story 7657 is IN PROGRESS (claimed, assignee session-log). The IMPLEMENTATION edits are DONE but UNCOMMITTED + UNTESTED in the working tree (main @ 25da9388). A resuming session: run `git status` / `git diff` to see the 8 changed files (listed below), then continue from "REMAINING STEPS". If the working tree was lost (fresh clone), re-apply the edits from "EDITS MADE".

== STATE ==
- main @ 25da9388 (config epic a621 fully closed). 8 files modified, uncommitted, NOT yet smoke-tested / unit-tested / reviewed / suite-run / committed.
- No active /loop cron (cancelled). Assignee used: "session-log".

== DESIGN (locked) ==
Add `exclude_session_logs: bool` to reduce_all_tickets and turn it ON for the dependency-graph/health hot paths + default `list`, while KEEPING session_log in `search` and single-ticket `show`. session_log was registered in add5 (CLOSED, commit 1327adf4): create works; claim/transition refused; blocking links refused; gates exempt.

== EDITS MADE (all uncommitted) ==
1. src/rebar/reducer/_api.py — reduce_all_tickets() gained `exclude_session_logs: bool = False` (+ docstring) and a post-filter after the exclude_deleted block: `if exclude_session_logs: results = [r for r in results if r.get("ticket_type") != "session_log"]` (error dicts w/o ticket_type preserved). Mirrors exclude_archived/exclude_deleted.
2. src/rebar/graph/_ready.py (find_ready_tickets) — reduce_all_tickets(str(tracker_dir), exclude_session_logs=True).
3. src/rebar/graph/_graph.py (_compute_dep_graph → backs `deps`/build_dep_graph) — added exclude_session_logs=True.
4. src/rebar/graph/_blockers.py (_find_direct_blockers) — added exclude_session_logs=True.
5. src/rebar/graph/_unblock.py (batch_close_operations) — added exclude_session_logs=True. IMPORTANT rationale: also prevents a lifecycle-exempt session_log child (which can never be closed) from counting as a perpetual "open child" that would block its parent epic's close.
6. src/rebar/graph/_hierarchy.py (compute_archive_eligible) — added exclude_session_logs=True.
7. src/rebar/_engine_support/next_batch.py (compute) — added exclude_session_logs=True.
8. src/rebar/_engine_support/reads.py (list_states) — reduce_all_tickets(..., exclude_session_logs=(ticket_type != "session_log")). ticket_type defaults to "" (exact single-type match, no comma list), so default list EXCLUDES session_log and only `list --type=session_log` INCLUDES it.

== DELIBERATELY NOT CHANGED ==
- reads.py search_state (~line 362): keeps exclude_session_logs default False → session_log stays in the search corpus. DO NOT add the exclusion here.
- show / single-ticket reduce path: unchanged (show returns a session_log fully).
- validate (_engine_support/validate.py): NO direct reduce_all_tickets call — it reads via _raw_tickets() → reads.list_states(tracker) with no type filter, so it INHERITS the list exclusion (session_logs are never health-flagged). No separate validate edit needed.
- src/rebar/_engine_support/descendants.py: OUT OF SCOPE (hierarchy enumeration; left alone).
- src/rebar/_commands/delete.py scan_and_write_unlinks: left alone ON PURPOSE — session_logs CAN have relates_to/discovered_from links, so their UNLINK cleanup must still run when a referenced ticket is deleted; excluding them would orphan links.

== REMAINING STEPS (resume here) ==
1. Smoke-test end-to-end via the library API in a temp initialized repo (rebar.init_repo): create a few task/story tickets + 1-2 session_log tickets with a distinctive keyword in the body; assert: rebar.list_tickets() omits session_log; list_tickets(ticket_type="session_log") returns them; rebar.search("<keyword>") matches the session_log body; rebar.show_ticket(<log>) returns it fully; rebar.ready_tickets()/next_batch()/deps()/validate() never include the session_log; validate() does not flag the session_log as orphan/empty.
2. Add a unit/interface test file asserting BOTH the exclusions and inclusions (suggest tests/interfaces/queries/ or tests/unit/ alongside reduce_all_tickets tests; mirror the established fixture pattern — rebar_repo for interface tier, or graph _helpers._write_ticket with ticket_type="session_log" for the reducer/graph unit tier). Cover: reduce_all_tickets(exclude_session_logs=True) drops session_log + keeps error dicts; ready/next_batch/deps/validate exclusions; default list omit + --type=session_log include; search includes; show includes. A perf-isolation assertion (graph timings unaffected by log size/count) is in the AC — at minimum assert COUNT-invariance (graph/ready results identical with vs without N session_logs present).
3. Dispatch a subagent code review of the diff; remediate.
4. Full offline tier: .venv/bin/python -m pytest -m "not integration and not external"  (baseline was 2049 passing at 25da9388).
5. uvx ruff check src tests + uvx ruff format --check src tests.
6. rebar comment 7657 (progress) + commit scoped to main + push.
7. rebar transition 7657-4ba6-bcd4-4698 in_progress closed; keep store health clean (rebar fsck + rebar validate).

== AC TO SATISFY (from the ticket) ==
- ready/next_batch/deps/validate never include session_log + timings unaffected by log count/size (asserted).
- default list omits session_log; list --type=session_log returns them.
- search matches session_log bodies; show returns a session_log fully.
- validate does not flag session_log as orphaned/empty/etc.
- tests assert both exclusions and inclusions.

== EPIC 7738 CONTEXT (for whoever continues the epic after 7657) ==
add5 CLOSED (foundation, 1327adf4). 7657 IN PROGRESS (this). Remaining OPEN siblings, all unblocked by add5: 1368 (exclude session_log from Jira reconcile — outbound_differ EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES), e2e3 (recent session-logs command across lib/CLI/MCP), e7e4 (session-log capture helper, all 3 interfaces), 8e10 (docs: type + exemptions + title-summary convention). Config epic a621 is fully CLOSED. Non-blocking follow-up 3b92 (route bridge_fsck's jira config) still OPEN, discovered_from 0ac6.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
RESUMED + COMPLETED (session-log loop). Implementation (8 files, exclude_session_logs flag) verified end-to-end via library smoke test: default list omits logs, list --type=session_log returns them, search/show include them, ready/next_batch/deps/validate exclude them.

Added tests: tests/interfaces/queries/test_session_log_visibility.py (9 interface-tier cases incl. perf count-invariance) + tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_exclude_session_logs.py (flag drops only logs / preserves error dicts).

Caught + fixed a regression: excluding session_logs from _compute_dep_graph dropped the queried log from the node set, so deps(<session_log>) lost its own relates_to/discovered_from links (broke add5's test_nonblocking_links_allowed). Fix in graph/_graph.py: when the queried ticket is absent from the log-excluded node set, reduce it singly so deps(<log>) still surfaces its non-blocking links — logs remain excluded as nodes/children/blockers of other tickets.

Full offline tier green: 2061 passed, 9 skipped, 0 failed (baseline 2049). ruff check + format clean.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
